behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between manifest validation and module loading/execution. A unit test of the validator alone can’t prove plugin code was never imported. Use real loader wiring with a test plugin whose module has an import-time side effect, and assert rejection happens with no side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one plugin whose manifest omits a required field and whose entry module writes a marker file (or flips shared state) at import time. Run the real loader on that directory. Assert: loader reports manifest validation failure, plugin is not loaded, and marker was never created/changed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module ordering behavior: manifest dependency parsing + graph resolution + hook invocation sequencing. A unit test of topological sort alone misses real handoff bugs in the loader pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create temp plugins A and B with manifests where A depends on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared log. Run real loader and assert load log order is ["B","A"].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle handling is at the dependency-graph/loader boundary. Unit-testing cycle detection logic is useful but insufficient for this behavior, which also requires asserting that neither plugin is actually initialized by the runtime loader path.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A; each entry/onLoad writes a distinct marker. Run loader and assert: cycle error is reported with both plugin names, no onLoad calls occurred, and no markers were written.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This verifies runtime sequencing across module import execution and lifecycle hook dispatch. A unit test of hook runner cannot catch real ordering bugs introduced by module loader integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets `globalThis.pluginInit = true` during module evaluation, and whose onLoad asserts that flag is already true (or logs observed value). Run loader and assert onLoad executes and observes initialized state; fail if onLoad ran first.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestrated error-isolation behavior across dependency graph evaluation and lifecycle execution. A unit test of try/catch logic misses whether the real loader correctly skips dependents while continuing independent plugins.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: F (throws in onLoad), D (depends on F), and I (independent). Run loader and assert: F error is reported, D is not loaded, I onLoad is still called successfully.
